content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactually-Augmented Dynamic Prompting for Bias Reduction in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often reproduce social biases and stereotypes in their outputs, even when given explicit instructions to avoid them. Current prompting techniques only partially mitigate these artifacts and lack robustness across contexts.",
  "motivation": "Reducing bias in LLMs is crucial for equitable AI deployment in sensitive domains such as hiring, education, and healthcare. Existing prompt engineering and instruction-following approaches are brittle and highly context-dependent, necessitating more adaptive and principled prompting methods. An effective, generalizable prompting method would represent a major advance toward safe, fair language model use.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual Dynamic Prompting (CDP): for any input, we generate a set of counterfactual variants by systematically swapping demographic attributes (e.g., gender, race, age) using a counterfactual augmentation model. During inference, we prepend a meta-prompt instructing the LLM to compare its responses across all counterfactuals and to produce an answer invariant to these demographic changes. This is done in a single step via a specialized meta-instruction and an internal self-consistency check. The prompt is dynamically constructed for each scenario, ensuring robustness to varied stereotypes.",
  "experiment_plan": "Evaluate on StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, and WinoBias datasets. Compare CDP against standard prompts, prompt tuning, and instruction prompts using bias metrics (stereotype score, leakage) and task accuracy. Human evaluation will further assess stereotype mitigation and response fidelity."
}
